candidate_feature_families_conceptual: trailing price-derived state of market m up to t (e.g., trailing realized volatility, trailing range/ATR-like dispersion, momentum/trend descriptors) -- all windows END at or before t
cross-market state up to t (e.g., trailing co-movement/correlation among ES/GC/CL/6E, relative dispersion) computed only from <= t data
term-structure / roll-state descriptors available at t (if continuous-contract construction exposes them) -- point-in-time
calendar / seasonality features knowable at t (day-of-week, month, pre-announced event-window flags from a point-in-time calendar)
allowed_decision_time_features.feature_windows_must_end_before_label_wi: Every feature window must close at or before t; the label window opens strictly after t. No overlap.
allowed_decision_time_features.principle: A feature is allowed ONLY if it is fully computable from information available at or before the decision time t (point-in-time correct). All features are indexed at t and use data with timestamp <= t.

contamination_firewall.rule: This branch is self-contained. It does NOT import, reference, or inherit SPARTA_BRAIN weekly relative-strength rotation, FRC, BLOCKED_AT_6_GATES, the DR/K gate framework, Strategy-Lab promotion, or any other cross-project mechanic/universe/gate vocabulary. Its lifecycle, gates, and terminology are its own.

coverage_required.discovery: full 2024 + sufficient PRIOR history so the longest feature lookback is fully warmed at the first 2024 decision date
coverage_required.oos: full 2025 (SEALED; availability may be audited but values not inspected for labeling/fitting)

data_requirements.frequency: daily (pre-committed; intraday out of scope unless a later step re-scopes)
data_requirements.instrument_type: futures continuous-contract series (construction/roll convention to be FIXED and documented; e.g., volume/OI-based roll, ratio- vs difference-adjustment) -- point-in-time correct
data_requirements.no_fetch_in_this_branch_step: Steps 01-03 do NOT fetch ES/GC/CL/6E data; data requirements are a SPEC for a later separately-authorized acquisition step.
data_requirements.point_in_time: all series must be reconstructable as-of any t without future information; revisions/rolls applied point-in-time

discovery_2024_vs_oos_2025_constraints.discovery_2024: ALL design happens here: target threshold/horizon selection, feature definition, any future model fitting, inspection, and validation-internal splits (e.g., within-2024 cross-validation).
discovery_2024_vs_oos_2025_constraints.firewall_enforcement: discovery/OOS separation is a hard gate audited at Step 03 (enforceability) and re-checked before any OOS step.
discovery_2024_vs_oos_2025_constraints.oos_2025_sealed: 2025 is a write-once holdout. It is NOT inspected, fitted, thresholded, or used for any decision until a SEPARATE pre-registered OOS-evaluation gate is authorized. No 2025-derived statistic may leak into discovery.
discovery_2024_vs_oos_2025_constraints.pre_registration: Before touching 2025, the target rule, horizon H, feature set, and evaluation metrics must be FROZEN/committed; OOS is then a single evaluation, not an iterative tuning set.

must_verify: A3.1 DATA AVAILABILITY/COVERAGE: for ES/GC/CL/6E, the SPEC'd fields exist at daily frequency over the discovery period (2024 + prior warmup); and that 2025 exists as a holdout (availability only -- NOT inspected for labels).
A3.2 POINT-IN-TIME / ROLL CORRECTNESS: continuous-contract construction + any adjustments are point-in-time, with the roll convention documented and free of look-ahead.
A3.3 CALENDAR ALIGNMENT: the 4 markets' trading calendars are reconciled (shared decision-time index defined; mismatches handled by a pre-committed rule, not silent fill).

A3.4 TARGET COMPUTABILITY/DEFINEDNESS: the label rule is unambiguous; horizon H fits within available history at every discovery decision date; the discovery base-rate is estimable and non-degenerate -- WITHOUT computing returns/PnL (label feasibility only).
A3.5 FEATURE DECISION-TIME VALIDITY: each allowed feature family is confirmed computable from <= t data only; windows end before the label window; no forbidden/leaky feature is present.
A3.6 LEAKAGE FIREWALL: feature/target time-separation and the discovery/OOS (2024/2025) firewall are enforceable and documented.

A3.7 SCOPE/GUARD COMPLIANCE: confirm NO model trained, NO clustering, NO signals, NO backtest, NO returns/R/PnL/Sharpe, NO tradeability/OOS/live/profitability claims, and NO SPARTA cross-project contamination.
exact_step_03_audit_requirements.output: exactly two files: reports/branch_multi_market_bad_regime_predictor_step_03_data_feature_target_audit.json and .md, grounded in THIS Step 02 document (cite its commit + content_sha256). Commit only if separately authorized.
exact_step_03_audit_requirements.purpose: Step 03 is an AUDIT that data, features, and target are sound and the methodology is enforceable -- with NO model/cluster/signal/backtest/returns and NO data inspection beyond integrity/availability needed for the audit.

forbidden_features_and_leakage_rules: NO future data: no value with timestamp > t may enter any feature.
NO target leakage: features must not be derived from, or be a deterministic function of, the label window (t, t+H] or the label itself.
NO contemporaneous-bar leakage: if the decision is made on bar t's close, features must not use any same-bar information unavailable at that close, nor any t+1.. information.

NO look-ahead in construction: continuous-contract rolls, revisions, or any data only knowable later must be applied point-in-time (no fully-revised/back-adjusted series that bakes in future rolls in a way unavailable at t).
NO OOS peeking: 2025 data must not inform any feature/target/threshold/model decision during discovery.
NO survivorship / selection on outcome: thresholds, horizons, and feature sets are pre-committed on discovery, not chosen by their OOS effect.
NO cross-project contamination: no SPARTA weekly-RS/FRC/gate-derived features or labels.
objective: For each market m in {ES, GC, CL, 6E} and each decision time t, produce a calibrated estimate (and binary label) of whether m will be in a 'bad regime' over the forward horizon H, using ONLY information available at or before t. Goal = predictive quality (classification), explicitly NOT trading signals or P&L.
supersedes_missing_reference: Replaces the previously-cited but non-existent Step 02 commit 2a85b96; THIS committed document is the new source of truth for Step 03.

target_definition.computation_status: NOT COMPUTED in Steps 01-03. No returns/R/PnL/Sharpe are computed. Step 03 audits only that the label is well-defined, unambiguous, and COMPUTABLE-IN-PRINCIPLE from the specified data (horizon fits available history; base-rate estimable on discovery only). Actual label realization is deferred to a later separately-authorized step.
target_definition.framing_note: The adverse-condition is a property of the MARKET STATE (a labeling statistic), not the return of any strategy. This branch predicts regimes; it does not trade them.
target_definition.horizon_H: a single, pre-committed forward horizon (e.g., a fixed number of trading days), fixed before any labeling; H is a design constant, not searched per result.
target_definition.label_rule_conceptual: y_{m,t} = 1 ('bad regime') iff the realized forward window (t, t+H] of market m satisfies a PRE-COMMITTED adverse-condition rule; else 0. The adverse-condition rule is a labeling function over realized market-state statistics (e.g., elevated realized volatility and/or a large sustained adverse price excursion over the window), with thresholds PRE-COMMITTED and estimated ONLY on discovery (2024) data.
target_definition.label_uses_future_window_BY_DESIGN: The TARGET is allowed to use the forward window (t, t+H] -- that is what makes it a supervised label. The FEATURES must not (see leakage rules). The label timestamp is anchored at t; the realization window is strictly after t.
target_definition.type: binary classification label per (market, decision-time t)

validation_gates_branch_own.G1_data_integrity_point_in_time: data is complete, calendar-consistent across the 4 markets, and point-in-time correct (no future leakage via rolls/revisions).
validation_gates_branch_own.G2_no_leakage_feature_target_separation: every feature window ends <= t; the label window is strictly after t; no feature is a function of the label window.
validation_gates_branch_own.G3_target_well_defined_and_base_rate_san: the label rule is unambiguous, computable-in-principle, and its discovery base-rate is neither degenerate (≈0% or ≈100%) nor undefined.
validation_gates_branch_own.G4_discovery_only_fitting: all thresholds/feature/model choices are made on 2024 only; documented and frozen before OOS.
validation_gates_branch_own.G5_oos_sealed_until_preregistered: 2025 untouched until a separate pre-registered evaluation; no peeking.
validation_gates_branch_own.G6_honest_reporting_classification_only: results are reported as CLASSIFICATION quality (e.g., AUC, precision/recall, calibration, base-rate-relative lift) -- NEVER returns/PnL/Sharpe/tradeability/profitability. No live-readiness or OOS-confirmation language.
validation_gates_branch_own.note: These are THIS branch's gates; they deliberately do NOT reuse SPARTA DR/K/FRC/6-gate constructs.
